programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: Over-powerful generals and money corrupted the Roman Republic, with the empire burdened with civil war and violence, and murder run rampant in the streets. Against this backdrop, Julius Caesar, desperate for fame and honour, embarked on a brutal decade-long campaign to annex Gaul and build his own reputation

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: A look at Rome's most influential characters, as they led an empire slowly sliding to its own destruction, and the Barbarian leaders who brought about that destruction. In 9AD, a ferocious Germanic chieftain, Arminius, slaughtered the Roman General Varus' army at Teutoburg Forest in a landmark battle that would mark the edge of the empire

programme.title: Colosseum
programme.desc: Drama-documentary that uses the Colosseum to tell the thrilling story of the rise and fall of the Roman Empire. As the Roman Empire continued to expand, so did the diversity of exotic beasts brought back to entertain the Colosseum crowds. Over one million animals were slaughtered in the arena by trained beast masters, none more famous than Carpophorus

programme.title: Killer Kings
programme.desc: The case against Ivan IV, also known as Ivan the Terrible, the infamous tsar of Russia, whose reign was characterised by violence and a strong, autocratic rule. After leading a miserable childhood, he had his first rival put to death while aged just 13. He was Grand Prince of Moscow from 1533 to 1547 and Tsar of All the Russias from 1547 until his death.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological digs at sites dating back to the Viking age, offering an insight into how the seafaring warriors from Scandinavia lived across Britain. In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to discover what life was like during the Bronze and Iron ages before the Romans invaded. In Norfolk, she helps to uncover a prehistoric monument that has been hidden for 2,000 years, and examines the Hebridean skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies, learning each is made up of parts from different people

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to learn more about mankind's Stone Age ancestors, and travels to Jersey to meet a team hoping to shed new light on the Neanderthals. In the Preseli Hills in Pembrokeshire, she learns about an investigation into the mystery surrounding Stonehenge, and hears stories of cannibalism and the ritual use of human skulls during the Ice Age

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit archaeological digs across the country, beginning with a look at the work being carried out in the east of England. The presenters visit Norwich Castle Museum, where they find out about developments at Must Farm in Cambridgeshire, a hunt for Roman treasure in Colchester, a burial site at Abington, an Anglo-Saxon royal hall at Lyminge, the 16th-century Tudor Basing House, and a dig involving Emperor Nero at Silchester

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit the Dorset Country Museum, where they explore finds from archaeological digs across the west of Britain. The presenters examine objects found at a Bronze Age burial site in Barrowclump, a Roman army site called Durotriges and the site of an early hospital at Winchester, Hampshire. Plus, a hoard found by metal detectors revealing hidden secrets of childhood 1,000 years ago, and a Roman settlement at Ipplepen in Devon

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at the silent threat of submarines, from tests in the River Thames, to the German's deadly U-boat attacks in the Second World War

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the unsolved murder of Crimewatch presenter and newsreader Jill Dando, who was shot dead outside her house in Fulham, west London, in April 1999. Intense media coverage followed and several lines of enquiry were pursued, including a theory that she had been killed by a jealous ex-boyfriend and the idea that somebody had hired a hitman in revenge for a conviction linked to Crimewatch. Following a trial at the Old Bailey, 40-year-old Barry George was found guilty of Dando's murder, but a third appeal quashed his original conviction and resulted in his acquittal in 2009

programme.title: The UnBelievable with Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: A look at the some of the strangest and most ghastly jobs in history - from night soil men cleaning excrement off the streets of Victorian London to professional mourners hired to weep at funerals, to intrepid deer urine collectors

programme.title: The UnBelievable with Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Some of the world's most valiant figures have overcome the most treacherous, impossible and downright bizarre circumstances. Instances include a man who survived the apocalypse or the woman involved in not one, but three maritime disasters. Plus, the cat that proved to be unsinkable in battle. These are the stories of survival, success and reunion against all odds

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective, telling how the Nazis conquered Germany and then half of Europe within a few years of losing the First World War. This first episode recounts how the clash of right-wing and left-wing political convictions turned the Weimar Republic into a powder keg. The fascist movement then seized power in Italy in 1922

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: How the failed coup on November 9, 1923 eliminated Hitler and then movement of some 55,000 members. The Führer and his National Socialist German Workers' Party achieved a miserable result in the 1928 Reichstag elections

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective. This episode recalls how a social protest turned into a racist and anti-Jewish movement during the 1920s. The NSDAP deliberately used emotions to gain the loyalty of the people

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: Events of 1933 and 1934 when President Hindenburg signed a decree in 1933 that suspended freedom of speech. It would be one of his last actions before his death from lung cancer. Also in this era, the first concentration camps for political opponents were set up

programme.title: World War II with Tom Hanks
programme.desc: The actor looks back at Pearl Harbor. Japan escalates their war in China, drawing increasing US sanctions and widespread outrage. Driven by resource shortages and military ideology, Japan attacks Pearl Harbor in a move that will reshape the course of World War II

programme.title: World War II with Tom Hanks
programme.desc: The story of how America's battle to control the seas against the German U-boats in the Atlantic and Japan's Navy in the Pacific ultimately turned the tide of the war

programme.title: The UnBelievable with Dan Aykroyd

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts finds out about discoveries that shed light on the Tudor Age, visiting excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch and at his last home in Stratford-upon-Avon, where clues reveal his economical use of money. In Wales, she meets a team of archaeologists learning about the realities of Henry VIII's dissolution of the monasteries, while the history of a forgotten royal palace is uncovered on the banks of the Thames

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological digs at sites dating back to the Viking age, offering an insight into how the seafaring warriors from Scandinavia lived across Britain. In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: Commando Missions
programme.desc: Operation Frankton, of December 1942. The mission was for Royal Marines to place limpet mines on German ships in the occupied Bordeaux Harbour, 62 miles along the Gironde River. Five cockleshell canoes set off from submarine HMS Tuna at the mouth of the river. Only two reached the harbour and carried out the raid, escaping with the aid of the French Resistance. The damage to the ships and the resulting disruption to German supply lines had a significant impact on the war effort

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago

programme.title: Matsalu Moose: Wild Giants of the Baltics
programme.desc: Matsalu National Park at the Baltic Sea is known for being one of Europe's most important stopover places for migrating birds. However, few know that an exceptionally large moose colony also lives here. This documentary follows the moose through the eyes of two yearlings as they are chased away from their mother before she gives birth to the next calf

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Leading shark ecologist Mauricio Hoyos attempts a TV first as he attaches a camera to the dorsal fin of a great white shark off Guadalupe Island

programme.title: Wild Science

programme.title: Becoming Elephant: The Orphans of Reteti
programme.desc: Following the journey of orphaned elephants at Reteti Elephant Sanctuary, the first community-owned and operated elephant sanctuary in Africa

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: An exploration of the remote wonder of the Exuma Cays, the Bahamas' first ocean park - a largely unexplored region made up of hundreds of tiny islands

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part one of two. The reasons why young animals enjoy playing, and how it can teach them to survive in the wild. The programme explores how young elephants, which rarely leave their mothers' side for the first eight years of life, play to develop social skills, while macaques hone their mental agility by playing games with stones. Plus, a zoologist who looks after orphaned cheetahs reveals how play can help cubs learn to hunt

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers

programme.title: Bugs That Ruled the World
programme.desc: A global investigation into insect declines, exploring their diversity and their critical roles on the planet. Scientists reveal the beauty of bugs, from pollinators to insect assassins to the tiny clean-up crews that purify the planet

programme.title: Bugs That Ruled the World
programme.desc: Learning how bees, butterflies, moths and other pollinators keep the fields fruitful and exploring what life would be like if these busy bugs vanished

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: Following a female king cobra in the hot forest of Southern India as she sets out to find a mate, find food and lay eggs before the monsoon arrives

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: As the land dries up, matriarch elephant Anura races to get her herd to a distant watering hole. But her tiny grandson is struggling to keep up

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the challenges and freedom of animals that use the air to survive and thrive, with the opening edition demonstrating how different species use air, from rookies to the masters of the skies

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A look at the animals which take to the skies and therefore gain an advantage over their rivals in the eat-or-be-eaten game

programme.title: The Wild Wild East

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Jackson makes the ultimate sacrifice by exposing himself to a lethal dose of radiation after an accident involving weapons of mass destruction threatens thousands on an alien world. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson and Michael Shanks

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: The team is sent to rescue a scientist stranded on a moon under attack from Osiris and Anubis, while Commander Thor - who was previously presumed dead - is taken prisoner by the system lords. Sci-fi drama, with Richard Dean Anderson and guest star Anna-Louise Plowman

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jonas decides to finish what Daniel started, and the Stargate malfunctions, plunging Earth into jeopardy. Meanwhile, Teal'c returns home to attend his wife's funeral with his son Rya'c, who believes she would not have been killed if his father had refrained from joining the fight against the Goa'uld. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Part two of two. With the Stargate transformed into a time bomb and the team unable to contact its off-world allies for help, Earth's only hope lies in a desperate gambit that could result in the permanent loss of the portal. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part one of three. Wraith warships stage a deadly attempt to capture Atlantis, forcing the Stargate team to find a place to flee - and a way to destroy the city. As the countdown to destruction ticks away, McKay tries to activate the Ancients' orbital weapons platform. Starring Joe Flanigan, Torri Higginson and David Hewlett

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part two of three. The Wraith close in and the future of the city looks doomed, until help arrives in the form of the Stargate SG-1 vessel Daedalus, bringing a new military contingent from Earth. Joe Flanigan stars

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Part three of three. Colonel Caldwell takes charge of Earth's latest hi-tech battle cruiser and saves Major Sheppard from his suicide run on the Wraith ships. McKay and the team race to protect Atlantis before their enemies arrive to conquer the city. Starring Joe Flanigan, Mitch Pileggi and David Hewlett

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The battle cruiser Daedalus becomes infected with a virus that may allow the Wraith to take over the ship, and McKay fights to prevent this from happening. Meanwhile, a meeting at the SGC reveals the Pentagon's plans to replace Major Sheppard with Colonel Caldwell. David Hewlett stars

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Teyla and Sheppard are taken captive by a former soldier whose entire unit was wiped out by the Wraith. Caldwell warns that Ford should be treated as a threat to the base's security - and McKay soon has a close encounter with the infected lieutenant. Sci-fi drama, starring Joe Flanigan

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Glenn and Tara are determined to reach Terminus, but Abraham is reluctant. Daryl struggles as he tries to adjust to the Claimers' rules. Post-apocalyptic drama following the exploits of a former County Sheriff struggling to survive in the aftermath of a zombie epidemic. Starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: After spending so long on the run from both the re-animated dead and ruthless fellow survivors, Rick and his acquaintances hope to find peace at Terminus, the mysterious community welcoming any and all in need of shelter. However, lessons learned at Woodbury continue to weigh heavily on everyone's minds, and it remains to be seen whether the so-called sanctuary will deliver on its promise of safety. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: The Wild Robot (2024)
programme.desc: Following a shipwreck, a robot called Roz survives on an uninhabited island with the help of wild animals. Family animation, with the voices of Lupita Nyong'o, Pedro Pascal, Kit Connor and Bill Nighy

programme.title: The Wild Thornberrys Movie (2002)
programme.desc: The animal-loving family heads for Africa, where Eliza discovers unscrupulous poachers are plotting to kill an entire herd of elephants with the aid of a deadly electric fence, leaving her and ever-present pal Darwin to save the day. Animated adventure, with the voices of Lacey Chabert, Tim Curry, Tom Kane, Cree Summer, Lynn Redgrave and Jodi Carlisle

programme.title: Deep Sea (2023)
programme.desc: Shenxiu was abandoned by her mother when she was a child. Since then, she has bad dreams about a monster called Nightmare. One day, she travels on a cruise ship with her family. On a stormy night, she falls and breaks into the fancy Deep Sea restaurant. She meets the Chef, Nanhe, and embarks on a mysterious journey. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Brian Flaccus and Angela Malhotra

programme.title: Bee Movie (2007)
programme.desc: A bee wearies of a life dedicated to producing honey and decides to leave his hive. During his foray into the wider world, he befriends a florist. However, when he realises what becomes of the honey his species toils so hard to make, he resolves to sue the entire human race for stealing from his fellow creatures. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jerry Seinfeld, Renee Zellweger and Chris Rock

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs (2009)
programme.desc: An incompetent scientist finally invents something that works - a machine that turns water into food. When the device gets launched into the atmosphere, it has a strange effect on his town's weather, causing giant meals to fall from the sky to the delight of the residents - until it gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Bill Hader, Anna Faris and James Caan

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs 2 (2013)
programme.desc: A scientist believes he has saved the world from his invention, a machine that accidentally turned water into food. However, he learns the device is still active and has created a race of food/animal hybrids that he must stop from running wild across the world. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Bill Hader, Anna Faris and James Caan

programme.title: Dog Man (2025)
programme.desc: A half-human canine is sworn to protect and serve as he faces feline supervillain Petey the Cat. Animated comedy, with the voices of Pete Davidson, Poppy Liu, Lil Rel Howery and Isla Fisher

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: The Wild Robot (2024)
programme.desc: Following a shipwreck, a robot called Roz survives on an uninhabited island with the help of wild animals. Family animation, with the voices of Lupita Nyong'o, Pedro Pascal, Kit Connor and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018)
programme.desc: After being bitten by a radioactive arachnid, student Miles Morales develops incredible abilities, and becomes a version of Spider-Man. When a super collider device opens a gateway to other dimensions, Miles crosses paths with five counterparts to stop a threat for all realities. Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Shameik Moore and Nicolas Cage

programme.title: DC League of Super-Pets (2022)
programme.desc: Krypto the Super-Dog fights alongside Superman in Metropolis, thwarting the evil schemes of arch-nemesis Lex Luthor. Disharmony ripples across Metropolis just as Luthor's former laboratory guinea pig, Lulu, enacts her scheme to abduct the Man of Steel and members of the Justice League. Krypto temporarily loses his abilities and places his trust in a misfit team of newly superpowered animal shelter escapees to save the day. Animated comedy adventure, featuring the voices of Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart, Kate McKinnon, Vanessa Bayer and Natasha Lyonne

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: The Pirates! In an Adventure with Scientists! (2012)
programme.desc: The bungling leader of a band of inept buccaneers is determined to prove his worth to his rivals and win the coveted Pirate of the Year award. An encounter with the naturalist Charles Darwin gives him the chance to pull off a daring crime - if he can survive coming face to face with the pirate-hating Queen Victoria. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Grant, Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton and David Tennant

programme.title: Flushed Away (2006)
programme.desc: A pampered pet rodent is flushed down a toilet and ends up in a vast underground rat city in the sewers of London. He is befriended by a quick-witted rat who offers to help him find his way home, but first the hapless pair have to escape the clutches of an evil toad bent on ridding the rodent metropolis of its residents. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Deep Sea (2023)
programme.desc: Shenxiu was abandoned by her mother when she was a child. Since then, she has bad dreams about a monster called Nightmare. One day, she travels on a cruise ship with her family. programme.desc: A spoiled, selfish cat carelessly loses his ninth life, but winds up being reincarnated as a different animal in each of his next lives. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mo Gilligan, Simone Ashley and Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Pets on a Train (2025)
programme.desc: When a gang of animal crooks set out on what should be a simple con, they're swept into a train robbery. It falls to Falcon, a small-time raccoon thief, and Rex, a by-the-book police dog, to put their differences aside and protect the passengers aboard a runaway high-speed train. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Wyatt Bowen and Daniel Brochu

programme.desc: When a peaceful island is visited by mysterious green piggies, its up to Red, Bomb and Chuck, outcast flightless birds, to get to the bottom of the suspicious invaders. Animation, with the voices of Jason Sudeikis, Josh Gad, Danny McBride, Maya Rudolph and Bill Hader

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie 2 (2019)
programme.desc: Red joins forces with Leonard's team as the flightless birds and scheming green pigs take their war to the next level. When a new threat emerges that puts both Bird and Pig Island in danger, Red, Chuck, Bomb and Mighty Eagle recruit Chuck's sister Silver and team up with pigs Leonard, his assistant Courtney and techpig Garry to forge an unsteady truce and form an unlikely superteam to save their homes. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Peter Dinklage, Tiffany Haddish and Dove Cameron

Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Corpse Bride (2005)
programme.desc: Shy bridegroom Victor unintentionally gets himself hitched to a ghostly bride, who lures him down into the netherworld. But as he strives to find his way back to the land of the living, a treacherous aristocrat is scheming to marry Victor's flesh-and-blood fiancee. Tim Burton's stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Johnny Depp, Helena Bonham Carter, Emily Watson, Christopher Lee and Richard E Grant

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania (2012)
programme.desc: Dracula opens a luxury resort for monsters, to provide a refuge from terrified humans. His cosy sanctuary is disrupted when a normal man unwittingly checks in, and the situation is complicated when the unwanted guest takes a liking to the count's daughter. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 3: A Monster Vacation (2018)
programme.desc: Count Dracula and company participate in a cruise for sea-loving monsters, unaware that their boat is being commandeered by the monster-hating Van Helsing family. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Adam Sandler, Andy Samberg and Selena Gomez

programme.title: The Ant Bully (2006)
programme.desc: A friendless, moody 10-year-old spends his free time terrorising ants - until the tiny creatures take their revenge by magically reducing him to their own size and forcing him to learn how to contribute to colony life. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Julia Roberts, Nicolas Cage, Meryl Streep and Paul Giamatti

programme.desc: Ambitious fish Oscar dreams of escaping his humdrum undersea life. His wish comes true when a gangster shark keen to escape a world of organised crime seeks his help in faking his own death. But Oscar soon finds himself out of his depth as his fearsome reputation gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Smith, Robert De Niro, Martin Scorsese, Renee Zellweger, Angelina Jolie and Jack Black

programme.desc: Dom Littlewood looks at remarkable filmed evidence showing how police, businesses and the public are using new technology to catch crooks. programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions - Togha agus Rogha
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh present highlights from two decades of the legendary music show, featuring musical stars from Ireland, the UK and the US, including Rosanne Cash, Mary Chapin Carpenter and Teddy Thompson. Fans of Transatlantic Sessions can see Sarah Jarosz and Alison Krauss sing Run Away and Sharon Shannon and Jim Murray give an uplifting performance of Dreamcatcher - Off the Hook. Karen Matheson is joined by Cara Dillon to close the programme with a rousing performance of The Diamond Ring

programme.title: Abair Amhrán
programme.desc: Series celebrating songs from days gone by, filmed on location around the country

programme.title: Abair Amhrán
programme.desc: Series celebrating songs from days gone by, featuring performances by Antaine Ó Faracháin, Bríd Ní Mhaoileoin, Robbie McMahon and Fiona Kelleher

programme.title: Cúramóirí
programme.desc: Documentary delving into the heart of daily Irish life, illustrating the crucial work and life saving care provided by family carers, professional carers and dedicated organisations to those who need it the most. Dublin's Diarmuid Mac Dubhglais provides food for those who are hungry,Pádraig Ó hAiniféin from Corca Dhuibhne tells of his spinal cord injury and palliative care nurse at the Galway Hospice Úna O'Kane demonstrates her work

programme.title: Hector Caribbean
programme.desc: Hector Ó hEochagáin gets to grips with the multicultural influences of life in the Dominican Republic, starting with a visit to a local baseball club, a cornerstone of Dominican life. Next, he experiences the unique rhythms of 'son', a distinctive musical genre of the island, and he even shows off a few moves. Hector tours a plantain farm where he learns how this staple of Caribbean cuisine is grown, before visiting the border with Haiti, where he watches as hundreds of people cross the bridge between the two countries to engage in trade

programme.desc: The fishing community of Dingle peninsula, across the season, from March to October. programme.title: Goirt Órga

programme.title: Iománaíocht Hollywood
programme.sub-title: Cluichí Gaelacha ar scannán (Gaelic Games on the Big Screen)
programme.desc: An insight into how Ireland, Irish people and Gaelic games have been depicted in the cinema since the beginning of the last century. The first of two programmes examines the portrayal of Gaelic games in film from the silent period, through newsreels, short films and feature productions. Featuring rare and classic productions, combined with fascinating interviews with relevant sporting, film and academic contributors

programme.title: Cúl Stáitse - Oireachtas 2025
programme.desc: Since it first began over 125 years ago the Oireachtas festival has hosted some of the most prestigious competitions in traditional arts and has grown from an event frequented by the hundreds to now been one of the most important cultural events in Ireland. This year's festival in Belfast had over 1,000 competitors taking to the stage in song, music, dancing and storytelling competitions. This documentary follows the competitors, some taking to the stage for the first time, others returning veterans, while all strive for perfection and a place in the history books

programme.desc: A near-collision in a driveway due to alleged recklessness escalates into a heated exchange and vandalism.

programme.desc: A woman accuses her former friend of theft and an illegal lockout; her ex-friend claims she brought in an illegal tenant.

programme.desc: A woman is accused of illegally using a professional photo of her grandchild to make a statement.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: An attempt to reign in a roommate's sloppiness ends in an assault, an arrest and a plea bargain.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Boarded Barn Murders
programme.desc: Award-winning author Simon Toyne travels to Cheshire to investigate the opportunistic crimes that took place at a remote barn house in Congleton.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Tenant From Hell
programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell revisits the case of David Ellis, who murdered landlord Alec Warburton in Dolwyddelan, North Wales, before fleeing to Ireland.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Lonely Killer
programme.desc: Professor David Wilson revisits the case of Dennis Nilsen, who willingly confessed to the murder of 15 young men and delighted in sharing every detail with his interviewers.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Confession of Murder
programme.desc: The case of Katie Rackliff, who was murdered by Sharon Carr in Farnborough. Katie was found the next day, it took nearly five years - and Carr's own confession - to solve the case.

programme.desc: Sean Humphrey is found dead at home and police use forensic evidence to find a suspect. They uncover his identity, but catching him proves more difficult.

programme.title: Britain's Countryside Killers

programme.desc: The story of Angela Craig, who was poisoned to death by her husband putting cyanide in her protein shakes.

programme.desc: The tragic case of John Chipp who was thrown 85ft to his death after a night out in Sunderland celebrating his 18th birthday.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A teenage girl rams her mom's uninsured car into a neighbour's vehicle; her mother sues the neighbour for $5,000 for harassing them about it.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A drag racer with full custody of his two children acknowledges that he defrauded the government and accepted money from his ex for car parts.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man makes all kind of accusations against his ex-wife while suing her for breaking a lease and reneging on a loan to buy a Ford Mustang.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man living off of social security claims his ex-landlord illegally evicted him, damaged his property and filed false charges against him.

programme.title: Britain's Almost Perfect Murders
programme.desc: The story of police officer Martin Forshaw, who killed his fiance, Claire Howarth, just days before their wedding. His poor staging of a car crash led to a life sentence.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: The Head On The Beach
programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell revisits the case of the greed-driven Lithuanian ex-soldier Vitas Plytnikas and an accomplice who murdered Jolanta Bledaite in Scotland.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Murder: The Tattoo Clue
programme.desc: A look at the case of a woman's body being found at a pumping station in Hull. Senior Investigating Officer Paul Davison and his team are tasked with identifying her.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's UK Killer Evidence
programme.desc: The story of what happened when an attempt to detroy evidence and disguise the truth in New Cross was solved through forensic science.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's UK Killer Evidence
programme.desc: Sean Humphrey is found dead at home and police use forensic evidence to find a suspect. They uncover his identity, but catching him proves more difficult.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Serial Killer in the Making?
programme.desc: Jackie Malton revisits the case of Alun Kyte, the Midlands Ripper, who murdered two women near Leicestershire in 1993 and 1994.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Murder: The Tattoo Clue

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: With the aid of CCTV footage, Nick Knowles reports on the case of a British pensioner on the trip of a lifetime lost in the Australian Bush without food and water. Plus, a mother on the school run crushed by her own car and a champion British kite surfer injured when a stunt goes wrong

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A family on a safari holiday are attacked by an elephant, while a singer and her band flee as a fire engulfs the five-star hotel at which they are performing. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles reports on a dad trapped beneath a bus, and a professional surfer who collides with a jet ski as he rides a huge wave

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A building site worker digs up a Second World War bomb, leaving the experts to dispose of it while 3,000 Londoners are moved out of their homes. Plus, a postmaster takes evasive action as a car somersaults towards him on a country road, and a nurse calls for help after being nearly trampled to death in a field of cows

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch quickly realises that the boy who tried to avenge Anna's murder is his son. Ogden is stunned but eager to accept the boy out of love for Murdoch

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A vaudeville comic's death leads Murdoch to suspect that Charlie Chaplin was the intended target. Meanwhile, Henry Higgins is elated to hear Ruth's news

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Inspector Brackenreid learns that his youngest son, Bobby, is wanted for murder, while Murdoch and Crabtree suspect old enemies are returning to harm them

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, a fishing community is excited about the arrival of their festival queen for an annual celebration, but when her boat arrives with only her dead body on board, DI Mooney is left, yet again, with a baffling mystery to solve. What could a missing loaf of bread have to do with the crime? Meanwhile, Florence and Patrice look forward to their wedding, which is only a week away, but a huge strain is put on their relationship when the groom is drawn into the murder case as a former employer and long-time friend of the victim

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team are left stunned by another murder connected to the killing of festival queen Tiana. But there is a new lead - a phone call relating to their initial suspect - so DI Jack and his officers travel over to nearby Rosalie Island, where the owner and employees of a lucrative investment company all fall under suspicion as they are questioned about the mysterious call. Meanwhile, with emotions running high, Ruby encourages her uncle, Commissioner Patterson, to show his emotional side for once. Guest starring Angus Deayton and Saskia Reeves

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is determined to find out why the body of a young man was taken from his graveyard. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest sets out to solve the murder of a local aeronautics magnate's son, and disaster strikes when Lady Felicia loses her singing voice. Robert Daws guest stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The clerical sleuth encounters a voodoo priest who is intent on winning back the woman he loves - no matter the cost. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A groom is killed on his wedding day, and Murdoch soon learns the man was a homosexual and the marriage a sham, contrived to secure a substantial inheritance. programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After a mysterious thief keeps stealing valuable treasures from under his nose, Hercule Flambeau visits Kembleford to see if Father Brown can help him to eliminate the competition. Mark Williams and John Light star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The agents track down the killer of a man who believed he was abducted by aliens, and the case becomes more complicated when his body is stolen from the coroner's van. Van Pelt takes time off from the investigation to shop for a wedding dress. Crime drama, starring Simon Baker and Amanda Righetti

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: A stock market guru's fortune is stolen, setting Charlie and Rex on the trail of a very surprising thief

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Carrie goes undercover as a chemical engineer with a band of professional bank robbers planning a multimillion-dollar heist. She's set on unmasking the anonymous mastermind overseeing the operation, but Al keeps a close eye on his partner and former girlfriend, fearing she's in way over her head. Guest starring Sendhil Ramamurth (Heroes' Mohinder Suresh)

programme.title: Return to Paradise
programme.desc: Just before participating in the Dolphin Cove fun run, entrepreneur Curtis Faleafar calls Mackenzie to warn her that many lives are in danger. He later collapses, apparently poisoned, and Mackenzie is convinced his warning had some connection to his death. Away from matters of work, she also has to work out a way to stop local bathers taking shortcuts through her property. Detective drama, starring Anna Samson and Lloyd Griffith

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A groom is killed on his wedding day, and Murdoch soon learns the man was a homosexual and the marriage a sham, contrived to secure a substantial inheritance. The detective suspects the best man may be involved, but contemplates that the truth may be even more elaborate than he is imagining. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The agents track down the killer of a man who believed he was abducted by aliens, and the case becomes more complicated when his body is stolen from the coroner's van. Van Pelt takes time off from the investigation to shop for a wedding dress. Crime drama, starring Simon Baker and Amanda Righetti

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: A stock market guru's fortune is stolen, setting Charlie and Rex on the trail of a very surprising thief

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After a mysterious thief keeps stealing valuable treasures from under his nose, Hercule Flambeau visits Kembleford to see if Father Brown can help him to eliminate the competition. Mark Williams and John Light star in the crime drama

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Carrie goes undercover as a chemical engineer with a band of professional bank robbers planning a multimillion-dollar heist. She's set on unmasking the anonymous mastermind overseeing the operation, but Al keeps a close eye on his partner and former girlfriend, fearing she's in way over her head. Guest starring Sendhil Ramamurth (Heroes' Mohinder Suresh)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: Return to Paradise
programme.desc: Just before participating in the Dolphin Cove fun run, entrepreneur Curtis Faleafar calls Mackenzie to warn her that many lives are in danger. He later collapses, apparently poisoned, and Mackenzie is convinced his warning had some connection to his death. Away from matters of work, she also has to work out a way to stop local bathers taking shortcuts through her property. Detective drama, starring Anna Samson and Lloyd Griffith

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team are tested when a billionaire's fiancee falls from a balcony the day before her lavish wedding. Everything points to suicide - except for the fact that the victim only painted one fingernail. With guest appearances by Denis Lawson, Elizabeth Berrington and Jemima Rooper

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: A stock market guru's fortune is stolen, setting Charlie and Rex on the trail of a very surprising thief

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective's estranged father is suspected of murder after being found standing drunk next to a dead body. Murdoch questions his own impartiality after new evidence is found, while also experiencing flashbacks concerning the death of his mother after a family bust-up. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A murder plot emerges when a skeletal corpse falls through the ceiling and on to the stage during a performance of Macbeth. The body is identified as the former owner of the theatre who had died a year earlier, leading to the re-opening of his coffin. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The owner of an exclusive dating website is murdered, and although Jane is certain the victim's wife is to blame, she is released due to a lack of evidence. When holes begin to appear in her alibi, the consultant discovers a way to convict her of the crime, while the rest of the team learns that some of the women at the agency are filming their encounters with wealthy men so they can blackmail them. Crime drama, starring Simon Baker

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ria Lina hosts the comedy show filmed at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience before introducing stand-up routines by fellow comedians Lucy Beaumont and Josh Pugh

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The clan's visit to the Second World War graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start when Lucy discovers that her grandfather was not the man she thought he was. In short, this seems to be a piece of family history best left buried. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jamie MacDonald and ITV's Deputy Political Editor Anushka Asthana joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: Dave Gorman performs a series of witty stand-up jokes about the digital footprints people leave behind when surfing the internet

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie starts a wilderness group just for girls, in the hope of rivalling Ron's male club. Meanwhile, Ben receives a lesson in relaxation from Tom and Donna. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Aziz Ansari, Rashida Jones and Nick Offerman

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: As well as Heavy D, Nat has to deal with some voodoo magic, which all proves too much for her. Thankfully auctioneer Sean Kelly is on hand with an emergency vehicle

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Barry, Darren and Scotty get up front and personal, while Boudicca and Linda go nose to nose over an uplifting prize. Meanwhile, John gives Nat a hard time for blind bidding

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the team put track cars the Mitsubishi Evo VIII and the Subaru Impreza STi through their paces, Vauxhall's VX220 Turbo is road-tested and the search for the greatest car focuses on Stuart Hall's love of the Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud. Richard Whiteley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team explores driving safety, and Jeremy Clarkson goes on manoeuvres in a Hummer - a military-style jeep - and Sweden's contribution to the world of supercars is revealed. Neil Morrissey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and his wife Suki Webster are in the Lake District, the first time they have had a holiday there. Their first destination is Kendal, but first they have to negotiate their motorhome under a low bridge. Later they go to the Kendal mint cake factory, and after crossing Windermere by ferry from Bowness, they head to a beautiful campsite on Coniston Water

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A wanted fugitive tries to escape as officers take her fingerprints, and a doctor entering America is caught smuggling in prescription medication worth thousands of dollars

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Britain's most talented detectives tackle a dangerous arsonist in Bristol, a drug smuggling operation in Manchester, and a family committing shocking animal cruelty in Cheshire

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jamie MacDonald and ITV's Deputy Political Editor Anushka Asthana joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions about rocks, rolls and rock 'n' roll, leaving Bill Bailey, Katy Brand, Eshaan Akbar and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Laura Lexx and Glenn Moore. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Ed Gamble, Ed Byrne, Maisie Adam, Rhys James and Ria Lina join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: Dave Gorman focuses on the role faith plays in modern life as he performs a series of witty stand-up shows

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: The comedian performs another round of witty stand-up shows, beginning as he gets involved in shredding some naughty magazines, helps a gerbil to fulfil its destiny, and is surprised by the pictures that develop from an old-fashioned camera he finds

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: People forced out of their homes in Honduras by the notorious MS-13 gang plead their case for asylum in America. Officers discover 3,000lb of marijuana hidden in a cargo truck

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers put their lives on the line as they prepare to transfer two members of a cartel. In Laredo, a cop from Caracas, Venezuela, pleads his case for asylum in the United States

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: Having spent a series travelling across Spain and sampling classic dishes from the country's rural regions, Rick Stein draws culinary inspiration from that trip to create a series of dinner-table feasts that would befit any Spanish yuletide celebration. The chef prepares clams with serrano ham and oloroso sherry, lamb-stuffed aubergines with Moorish spices, chicken with saffron and pine nuts, and orange caramel creams

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Part one of two. The chef celebrates Christmas in his adopted county of Cornwall, exploring the music, food and drink of the region as he takes a break from his worldwide travel adventures. As well as joining in the ancient tradition of wassailing, he prepares a goose, tests out the ice rink at Eden and enjoys the singing of Port Isaac's Fisherman's Friends

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond visit Pendine Sands in Wales to race a BMW 6 Series, a Porsche 911 and a Jaguar XKR. Hatchbacks, including the Vauxhall Astra, Mazda 3 and VW Golf, are scrutinised, while Johnny Vaughan and Denise Van Outen are the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson test-drives the Nissan Cube and the Renault Clio 182, James May examines the Jaguar XJS, while Richard Hammond investigates alternative ways of fuelling cars and puts the Cadillac CTS through its paces. Terry Wogan is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul and Bob fish for Pike on the River Test in Hampshire, which may not be the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cottage where the pair are staying, they decide to invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. As the sun sets on the final night of their fishing expedition, Paul and Bob decide to write a eulogy for each on the riverbank

He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers look across the Channel to see how the French have influenced British food over the centuries. They prepare a roast chicken with 40 cloves of garlic, and a delicate mille feuille, and examine UK products in traditionally French areas of gastronomy, from award-winning English wines to Scottish garlic

programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A wanted fugitive tries to escape as officers take her fingerprints, and a doctor entering America is caught smuggling in prescription medication worth thousands of dollars

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Britain's most talented detectives tackle a dangerous arsonist in Bristol, a drug smuggling operation in Manchester, and a family committing shocking animal cruelty in Cheshire

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jamie MacDonald and ITV's Deputy Political Editor Anushka Asthana joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions about rocks, rolls and rock 'n' roll, leaving Bill Bailey, Katy Brand, Eshaan Akbar and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Laura Lexx and Glenn Moore. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Aisling Bea is joined by Alex Brooker and Katherine Ryan, while Rob Beckett teams up with Natalie Cassidy and Tom Allen to discuss the nation's favourite holidays. Jimmy Carr hosts

programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. programme.desc: Dave Gorman focuses on the role faith plays in modern life as he performs a series of witty stand-up shows

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: Having spent a series travelling across Spain and sampling classic dishes from the country's rural regions, Rick Stein draws culinary inspiration from that trip to create a series of dinner-table feasts that would befit any Spanish yuletide celebration. The chef prepares clams with serrano ham and oloroso sherry, lamb-stuffed aubergines with Moorish spices, chicken with saffron and pine nuts, and orange caramel creams

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Part one of two. The chef celebrates Christmas in his adopted county of Cornwall, exploring the music, food and drink of the region as he takes a break from his worldwide travel adventures. As well as joining in the ancient tradition of wassailing, he prepares a goose, tests out the ice rink at Eden and enjoys the singing of Port Isaac's Fisherman's Friends

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond visit Pendine Sands in Wales to race a BMW 6 Series, a Porsche 911 and a Jaguar XKR. Hatchbacks, including the Vauxhall Astra, Mazda 3 and VW Golf, are scrutinised, while Johnny Vaughan and Denise Van Outen are the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul and Bob fish for Pike on the River Test in Hampshire, which may not be the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cottage where the pair are staying, they decide to invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. As the sun sets on the final night of their fishing expedition, Paul and Bob decide to write a eulogy for each on the riverbank

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman is caught stealing milk from Nonnatus House, but she pleads for mercy from sisters Julienne and Monica Joan, explaining that she has been stealing milk as she is pregnant. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward sister is off sick, but a lift malfunction sees her spend the evening with mother-to-be Connie instead of her planned rendezvous with Cyril. Nurse Crane tries to help a woman who is newly arrived from East Pakistan, while Shelagh enlists Violet, Trixie, Sister Frances and Valerie to put on a charity fashion show to raise money for the incubator fund

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Dwelling Place
programme.desc: Feature-length adaptation of the best-selling novel, telling the tale of a 16-year-old struggling to keep her five brothers and sisters out of the workhouse with the help of a kindly carpenter. Starring James Fox, Tracy Whitwell and Edward Rawle-Hicks

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The impact of the war begins to be felt in Great Paxford as the women are forced to make sacrifices for the salvage effort. But soon matters on the personal front replace their efforts on the home front as Kate and Jack's relationship intensifies, Pat's marriage deteriorates and Claire finally plucks up the courage to ask Spencer out - unaware he is hiding a secret that could ruin their relationship before it has even started. Meanwhile, Alison resorts to desperate measures to pay a bill, and soon realises her actions will cost her much more. Wartime drama, with Claire Rushbrook, Daisy Badger, Rachel Hurd-Wood and Fenella Woolgar

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty investigates the apparent suicide of Robert's uncle, found hanged at his home, and discovers the whole village where he lived is gripped by superstition. Hetty begins to suspect that the villagers of Readsby - once a notorious haven for witches - are still engaged in satanic practices, and is determined to prove that they were somehow involved

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: The Met contact DI Gillespie when there are rumours of sightings of Baron Battenberg, a man who has been missing for more than five years. With the press and police in a frenzy, Sam and Ruth go undercover at a retreat run by the absent man's wife and brother to investigate. When an equally curious journalist is murdered, Sister Boniface is brought in to help with the inquiry. Lorna Watson stars

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

An outraged Ric demands she take the first flight back to the UK, declaring that he's needed where he is. But Diane is not convinced his reasons for staying are entirely altruistic, and investigates unresolved conflicts in his family concerning the death of his father. programme.desc: Barbara Dickson and Debbie Arnold guest star alongside Messrs Barker and Corbett, performing in a story called The Village of the Smiths

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including a Party sketch and An Appeal on Behalf of the Indecisive

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Air Studios, on the Island of Montserrat, which became a mecca for rock legends. Plus, Tskaltubo in Georgia, built to host a fearsome dictator

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: A look at the beauty of the region's forests and waterways through the seasons, with ingenious ravens working together to outsmart golden eagles to get a meal, while osprey swoop down to catch fish from lakes shaped by beavers. In the spring, a wandering male lynx leaves his scent on a twisted pine, hoping to attract a mate, while a mother bear emerges from hibernation and tries to teach her playful cubs to climb

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: The dramatic extremes of the Nordic territories - volcanic Iceland, the high tundra and Arctic Svalbard - where polar bears, arctic foxes and musk ox must all survive in a world that ranges from total darkness to midnight sun. For some, winter is about resilience and scraping through until the summer feasts, but for others it's the summer heat that brings the greatest danger.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Thelma looks on with disapproval as Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions to his new house, releasing a flood of nostalgic memories about their youthful misadventures. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry decides he would like to track down some of his former girlfriends - little suspecting the shocks that await him. James Bolam stars

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. They are on their way to meet experts who have discovered ancient cave art thought to be among the oldest on the planet. But they have also heard rumours that the mountain range contains further unmapped caves - and Steve wants to be the first to track them down, and whatever is hidden within

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist continues to explore the secrets behind evolution, this time focusing on the animal kingdom's bizarre hybrids, including the 'pizzly bear', a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which is thought to have come into being as a result of global warming. He also covers the killer bee brought into existence due to the transfer of African bees to South America

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: In Perth, the pressure is on for a team tackling a high-stakes engine rebuild, and in Townsville, a crew of experts build a massive underframe for a freight powerhouse

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A locomotive needs a quick double bogie change to get back to the sugarcane harvest. Meanwhile, the heat is on for a tricky air conditioner replacement

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A freight train needs an urgent engine replacement after a critical failure, and a crew works on a complex bogie change for a 132 T locomotive

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A busy commuter train undergoes a tough traction motor replacement, while a leaky torque seal sidelines a sugarcane locomotive urgently needed for the harvest

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry decides he would like to track down some of his former girlfriends - little suspecting the shocks that await him. James Bolam stars

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma throw a surprise party to cheer up Terry - but the blindfolded guest of honour begins to insult old friends, unaware they are listening to his every word

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Terry seek sanctuary from the news so they can watch TV highlights of an England v Bulgaria football match without knowing the result, but they are pursued by the relentless Flint, who has bet them they cannot escape the final score. James Bolam and Rodney Bewes star, with Brian Glover and Donald Gee

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: The ever-optimistic Bob tries to broaden Terry's social circle by inviting him to a trendy dinner party, but as usual his well-meaning efforts result in disaster. Rodney Bewes and James Bolam star as the original men behaving badly

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert
